summary: Rachel Torres and Sofia Petrov met with Victor Zheng of Frostbyte to discuss pain points after approximately eight months on Aegis Identity. Victor highlighted two key product gaps: the lack of hierarchical role inheritance in the flat role model, which causes manual permission duplication and complicates their ongoing SOC 2 audit, and the inability to configure session timeout policies per role or resource. Victor also praised Aegis Comply v2's on-demand SOC 2 reporting as a significant time-saver. He clarified he is not evaluating alternatives but wants assurance that Aegis Identity can scale with Frostbyte's rapid growth over the coming quarters.
actionItems: Rachel Torres: Follow up with product management to get a more concrete timeline on hierarchical role inheritance and share it with Victor
Rachel Torres: Formally submit both feature requests (hierarchical roles and granular session timeout policies) to the product team with the compliance audit context to support prioritization
Rachel Torres: Send a summary email to Victor recapping the discussion, feature requests, and next steps
Sofia Petrov: Verify current roadmap status of role-based or resource-based session timeout policies and relay findings to Rachel

keyMoments.text: Victor identifies the lack of nested role inheritance as the primary pain point, describing the current flat role model as error-prone and difficult to audit.
keyMoments.speaker: Victor Zheng

keyMoments.text: Victor praises Aegis Comply v2's on-demand SOC 2 report generation as saving his team real time during their active audit.
keyMoments.speaker: Victor Zheng

keyMoments.text: Sofia and Rachel acknowledge that the flat role structure creates compounding noise in compliance reporting, agreeing to flag the audit angle to boost product priority.
keyMoments.speaker: Rachel Torres

keyMoments.text: Victor raises a second feature gap: the inability to set session timeout policies per role or resource, requiring the most restrictive global policy to apply across all environments.
keyMoments.speaker: Victor Zheng
